items.title: Man jailed for assaulting off-duty police officer in Newtown has sentence reduced
items.summary: Koby Weaver, 21, was sentenced last month to two years' jail, after he ran into a brawl between two of his friends and two off-duty officers.

items.title: Make the most of cheap and versatile tinned beans with these recipes
items.summary: From vegetarian chilli to hearty soup, these easy recipes might convince you to add beans to your regular dinner rotation.

items.title: State of Origin: Why did Billy Slater leave Reece Walsh out of his Game I Queensland Maroons team?
items.summary: Reece Walsh's generational run to end 2025 was almost without peer, but he has rejoined the ranks of the mortals to start this year.

items.title: Vitrinite employees owed $16 million as Vulcan mine bids come due
items.summary: Federal Court documents show employees of the Vitrinite group of companies are collectively owed more than $16 million in entitlements. The mine near Dysart in Central Queensland has faced ongoing financial difficulties, exacerbated by lower coal prices.

items.title: Nicola Spurrier shares road safety message after she was hit by car while cycling
items.summary: SA public health chief Nicola Spurrier opens up about the moment when she was knocked from her bike by a car last year, and recounted the experience — which left her with broken bones and fearing for her life — only hours after a 39-year-old motorcyclist was killed in a crash in the Adelaide Hills.

items.title: Man pleads not guilty to murder but guilty to manslaughter in park amputation trial
items.summary: The trial of a man who allegedly performed an amputation on a 66-year-old, causing his death, is likely to focus on the accused's "state of mind" and intention, a court hears.

items.title: Horror MotoGP crashes at Catalan Grand Prix leaves rider with broken neck
items.summary: Alex Márquez is lucky to be alive after he spectacularly wrecked his MotoGP bike during the Catalan Grand Prix in Barcelona, while Johann Zarco was also taken to hospital after an equally terrifying crash later in the race.

items.title: Federal treasurer orders more Chinese-linked investors to offload shares in Australian rare earths mine
items.summary: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.

items.title: Corbie Walpole, 25, appeals jail term for drunkenly setting friend on fire
items.summary: Corbie Walpole's legal team has argued the sentencing judge failed to properly consider psychiatric evidence about her mental health at the time of the offending.

items.title: Prime minister backs Whyalla steelmaking as work continues to bring blast furnace 'back to life'
items.summary: Anthony Albanese says he remains "very confident" in the future of steelmaking in Whyalla, and says the federal government is "continuing to work" with lead producer Nyrstar to secure its operations in Port Pirie and Hobart.

items.title: WA mouse plague pushes regulator to approve stronger bait
items.summary: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

items.title: Mice fill seeding bins as WA farmers try to plant the 2026 crop.
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Live frog found in bag of lettuce in remote Western Australian town
items.summary: A Kiwi couple in a remote port town on WA's south coast have shared an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.

items.title: Court mention for 33-year-old charged with murder over Gooburrum death
items.summary: Yutthawichan Phetrchaisak is remanded in custody after being charged with the murder of a 42-year-old man north of Bundaberg.

items.title: Steven Miles squeaks a win in Stafford by-election, despite drop in Labor vote
items.summary: Labor candidate Luke Richmond will become the next member for Stafford, but the party's vote dropped several points.

items.title: Taiwanese president says island will not be sacrificed after Trump-Xi meeting
items.summary: Speaking after Donald Trump's meeting with China's president, Taiwan's leader says the island will not be sacrificed or traded and will not give up its free way of life.

items.title: Fashion designer Rochelle Gregory uses film to untangle her mental health battle
items.summary: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

items.title: 1.2 million Queensland homes unprepared as smoke alarm deadline nears
items.summary: A national company estimates many Queensland homes are unprepared as the deadline to install compliant smoke alarms nears.

items.title: ASX down after Wall Street losses, AUD also lower, oil rises — as it happened
items.summary: The ASX 200 finishes the first session of the trading week with a fall of 1.45 per cent, continuing its downward trend this month. Meanwhile, uncertainty over the Iran war has sent Brent crude oil above $110 a barrel.

items.title: Albanese rejects claim discretionary trust plan amounts to 'death tax' — as it happened
items.summary: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has rejected suggestion the government's planned tax on discretionary trusts amounts to a so-called "death tax".

items.title: No Walsh for Queensland as Blues coach accidentally lists former Maroon
items.summary: Billy Slater leaves Reece Walsh out of his squad for State of Origin I, while coach Laurie Daley accidentally announces the name of an ex-Queensland player.

items.title: New South Wales animal rescuers call for change amid a growing stray cat 'crisis'
items.summary: Animal shelters across New South Wales sound the alarm on a worsening stray cat crisis.

items.title: Central Australian Aboriginal ranger camp celebrates importance of women in protecting country
items.summary: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

items.title: Criminal cases at risk of being halted as NT legal aid crisis leaves people without lawyers
items.summary: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.

items.title: French probe into Jeffrey Epstein sees about 10 new alleged victims come forward
items.summary: About 10 new suspected victims have come forward as part of a French probe into the network of late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Elk sighting in SA's south-east raises questions about deer farm escapes
items.summary: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.

items.title: Iran weekly briefing: A new front emerges, plus the fallout from Trump-Xi and whispers of renewed strikes
items.summary: The UAE shifts from target to combatant amid reports the US and Israel are preparing to renew strikes on Iran, writes Matthew Doran.

items.title: Whyalla's steel and Port Pirie's lead are facing a make-or-break six months
items.summary: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.

items.title: Collingwood sobering-up centre to close less than four years after public intoxication reforms
items.summary: A 20-bed facility in the Melbourne suburb of Collingwood will close in 2027 after operating for just a few years following legal reforms.

items.title: Developers with state-tied projects donate to Queensland politicians after ban lifted
items.summary: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.

items.title: Wallabies and All Blacks face similar dilemma when players head overseas
items.summary: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.

items.title: Ex-NSW Labor minister and NDIS campaigner says Albanese government can't rush scheme changes
items.summary: A former NSW Labor powerbroker and state disability minister pivotal in advocating for the establishment of the NDIS has warned against rushing through sweeping changes to the scheme.

items.title: Albanese and Taylor both looking to history for future of Australia's tax system
items.summary: The Albanese government and the opposition both want to turn back the clock on the Australian tax system.

items.title: Australia's interest rate rise comes as other countries maintain theirs. Why?
items.summary: As Australians pay more on their mortgages, some countries have had no rise in their interest rate in recent years. So what's going on?

items.title: Push for Australia to extract helium gas as Middle East war impacts global supply
items.summary: Helium is used in health care and to make microchips and semiconductors, but the Middle East war has affected supply. Some think it is an opportunity for Australia's gas industry.

items.title: UAE slams 'dangerous escalation' after drone hits near nuclear plant
items.summary: Abu Dhabi officials say radiation levels are normal and there was no damage to the plant, but authorities are now working to determine who carried out the attack.

items.title: Reasons it's hard to end a long-term relationship, even when you want to
items.summary: It's not just practical and emotional reasons that get in the way of executing a break-up. There are many ways your brain is making you want to stay.

items.title: Andrew McLachlan condemns party's stance on permanent migrants
items.summary: Senator Andrew McLachlan raises concerns over Angus Taylor's use of the term "mass immigration" and plan to push tax-paying migrants off welfare benefits.

items.title: Older Australians concerned about Labor's plan to reduce private health insurance rebates
items.summary: The federal government plans to reduce private health insurance rebates for older Australians, a move one advocate fears could "tip many over the edge".

items.title: The ripples of the Iran war are everywhere
items.summary: It has been more than two months since the US and Israel launched strikes in Iran. But the impact of the conflict continues globally.

items.title: Dave Hughes on family, sobriety and creating laughter until his dying day
items.summary: Comedian Dave Hughes spoke to The Assembly about why he quit alcohol at 22, being "addicted to work" and the type of dad he's trying to be for his three kids.

items.title: This week in history quiz, May 18: Where was Pac-Man first released?
items.summary: In what year did John Howard become a federal MP? And which country got to play Pac-Man first? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

items.title: Six stores ordered to shut as WA's illegal tobacco laws come into force
items.summary: More than 140,000 illegal cigarettes have been seized and six stores ordered to close down, days after Western Australia's new tobacco laws came into force.

items.title: Perth father Steven Mattaboni identified as Rottnest shark attack victim
items.summary: The wife of 38-year-old Perth man Steven Mattaboni says her family's "hearts are irrevocably broken" after he was killed in a shark attack while spearfishing off the coast of Rottnest Island on Saturday.

items.title: Blues suffer Origin blow, Ponga will 'play anywhere' for Maroons
items.summary: NSW has been dealt a State of Origin blow ahead of the Blues' team announcement on Monday, with Latrell Mitchell ruling himself out of Game I selection.

items.title: Harley heroics lift Eagles to stunning upset win over Giants
items.summary: West Coast surges to a third victory of the season, defeating GWS by 17 points while St Kilda and Fremantle also enjoy Sunday wins.

items.title: Love story: How an Aussie rocker and a punk grandma found harmony
items.summary: Singer/guitarist Mick Medew and self-proclaimed punk grandma Ursula Collie were in their mid-50s when they met and bonded over a mutual love of live music. A decade on, they're musical and life partners.

items.title: Labor claims victory in Stafford by-election in Brisbane, LNP concedes candidate 'just won't get there'
items.summary: Labor candidate Luke Richmond claims victory over the LNP's Fiona Hammond in a tight race in the north Brisbane electorate of Stafford.

items.title: Jim Chalmers defends impact of tax changes on young investors
items.summary: Jim Chalmers says it is better for young people to invest based on economic rather than tax outcomes, and argued they can still access negative gearing by buying up new homes.

items.title: Reef restoration brings back marine life and boosts Swan River's health
items.summary: Work is underway to restore Perth's Swan River and help its severely degraded riverbed, where 1 million tonnes of limestone was taken in the 1990s.

items.title: Western Australia's first fainting goats settle into farm near Busselton
items.summary: The goats, which "faint" when stressed or excited, have travelled to a farm near Busselton, where they will be bred as pets.

items.title: Eurovision key moments: from Euro-pop to Euro-slop as Delta Goodrem makes Australia proud
items.summary: Slept through Eurovision this year? Here's what you missed from Europe's favourite song contest, with boycotts, ballads and a Bulgarian song called Bangaranga making headlines.

items.title: Manchester City wins FA Cup final over Chelsea, Pep Guardiola maintains Premier League trophy focus
items.summary: Manchester City's FA Cup celebrations after a 1-0 win over Chelsea will be low key, as the team refocuses on its tight Premier League title race with Arsenal.

items.title: First photo of wild Myrmeleon houstoni taken by unsuspecting Mildura woman on bushwalk
items.summary: There were no known photographs taken in the wild of the Myrmeleon houstoni until a regional Victorian woman snapped a photo of one while on a bushwalk.

items.title: Canberra family creates foundation for youth sports to honour daughter killed in bus crash
items.summary: Uqasha Imran was fatally injured when a bus driver struck her on her motorcycle in 2025. Now, her family are promoting the importance of organ donation while helping other young athletes like her.

items.title: Tasmanian town of Ross working to be accredited by Dark Sky International
items.summary: The northern Tasmanian town of Ross is working to protect its night sky, a years-long process that would see it formally recognised for its darkness and clarity.

items.title: Rider, Stacey Smith completes a full run at the Topar campdraft in April this year.
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Topar Campdraft Clinic riders take the reins and get hooked on the sport
items.summary: Amid the red dirt and flies of an outback stockyard, thrillseekers with a passion for horses learn about a quintessential Aussie sport.

items.title: APY Art Centre Collective calls for federal inquiry into First Nations art industry
items.summary: APY Art Centre Collective members say a voluntary code of conduct isn't protecting artists from financial exploitation and misconduct in the industry.

items.title: Could wine provide Australia's next source of biofuel?
items.summary: Australia's wine industry is investigating whether its current 263 million litre glut of wine would be better used as biofuel to power cars, trucks or even aircraft.

items.title: Victoria's political donation system remains a free-for-all ahead of election
items.summary: With only six months before the state election, the Victorian government struggles to devise new donation laws after the High Court found previous laws unconstitutional.

items.title: Gurrumul’s ARIA Hall of Fame honour brings pride and reflection for family
items.summary: Nearly a decade since his death, Gurrumul is set to be inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame, bringing pride and reflection to a remote part of Australia.

items.title: First home buyers buoyed by negative gearing, capital gains changes at first Saturday auctions since budget
items.summary: The government says the changes are aimed at levelling the playing field for young buyers bidding against property investors, while the opposition is pledging to undo the reforms, citing their potential to raise rents.

items.title: NSW government funds Aboriginal-led south coast sea urchin enterprise with $1.48m grant
items.summary: After decades of fighting for their traditional fishing rights, Aboriginal custodians are now fighting to save their sea country from ecological collapse.

items.title: Sam Kerr ties Chelsea's goals record in final Women's Super League match
items.summary: Sam Kerr ties the record for the most Women's Super League goals for Chelsea as she plays her final match with the London club after more than six years.
